title: Partition Equal Subset Sum
problem_statement: Given a non-empty array nums containing only positive integers, determine whether the array can be partitioned into two subsets whose sums are equal.

examples.explanation: The array can be partitioned into [1,5,5] and [11].
No partition produces equal subset sums.

intuition: If the total sum is even, the problem reduces to checking whether there is a subset that sums to total/2.

approaches.name: Recursive Brute Force
approaches.description: Try including or excluding each number and check whether a subset sums to half the total.
approaches.why_not: Exponential time due to trying every subset.

approaches.name: Memoized Recursion (Top-Down DP)
approaches.description: Cache whether a subset sum is achievable for each index and remaining target.
approaches.why_not: Bottom-up uses the same idea with less recursion overhead.

approaches.name: Bottom-Up Subset Sum DP
approaches.description: Use a boolean DP array to mark reachable sums and determine whether target/2 is achievable.
approaches.why_not: This is the standard optimal approach for subset sum problems.

interview_qa.a: If the total sum is odd, it cannot be split into two equal integer subsets.
It indicates whether a subset sum of j is achievable with the numbers processed so far.

interview_qa.q: Why iterate backward through the dp array?
interview_qa.a: To avoid reusing the same number more than once in the current iteration.

interview_qa.q: How does this relate to knapsack?
interview_qa.a: It is a 0/1 knapsack problem where each number can be chosen at most once to achieve a target capacity.
